name: Frontend, React, and Browser — Essential
description: Curated full-stack essentials selected from the complete pack. Use this as a lighter replacement for the full version.

topics.name: React Rendering Model
topics.description: Render, reconciliation, commit, and the difference between component execution and DOM updates.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

React calls component functions to create the next UI description, reconciles it with the previous result, and commits only required host changes.

## What problem does it solve?

Confusing a component render with a DOM update leads to mutation bugs, render side effects, and premature optimization.

## How it works

- State updates, parent renders, Context changes, and store subscriptions can trigger rendering.
- Render must be pure.
- Reconciliation matches old and new elements.
- Commit applies DOM changes and effects run afterward.
- A component re-run does not guarantee a DOM change.

## Common mistakes

- Network calls during render.
- Mutating state and expecting React to detect it.
- Assuming every re-render is expensive.

## Key terms

- render phase — computes the next UI
- reconciliation — matches element identity
- commit phase — applies host changes

hu.name: React Rendering Model
hu.description: A render, reconciliation és commit működése, valamint a component execution és DOM update különbsége.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

React component functionöket futtat a következő UI descriptionhöz, ezt reconciliation során összeveti az előzővel, majd csak a szükséges host change-et commitolja.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A component render és DOM update összekeverése mutation bugot, render side effectet és premature optimizationt okoz.

## Hogyan működik?

- State update, parent render, Context change és store subscription triggerelhet renderelést.
- A render pure legyen.
- A reconciliation régi és új elementet párosít.
- A commit alkalmazza a DOM change-et, majd effect fut.
- Component újrafutása nem jelent biztos DOM change-et.

## Gyakori hibák

- Network callt render közben indítani.
- State-et mutálni és React detectálását várni.
- Minden re-rendert drágának tekinteni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- render phase — a következő UI kiszámítása
- reconciliation — element identity párosítása
- commit phase — host change alkalmazása

topics.name: Reconciliation and List Keys
topics.description: Preserving component identity across list insertion, deletion, and reordering.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

React uses element type and sibling keys to decide whether an existing component instance still represents the same logical item.

## What problem does it solve?

Index or random keys can move local state and focus to the wrong row or remount every item.

## How it works

- Use a stable identifier from the data.
- Keys need to be unique among siblings.
- Changing a key intentionally resets component state.
- Index keys are safe only for static lists.

## Common mistakes

- Using indexes for reorderable lists.
- Generating random keys during render.
- Expecting key as a normal child prop.

## Key terms

- key — stable sibling identity
- remount — replacing a component instance
- positional identity — identity based on position

hu.name: Reconciliation és List Keys
hu.description: Component identity megőrzése insertion, deletion és reorder közben.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

React az element type és sibling key alapján dönti el, hogy a component instance ugyanazt a logikai itemet képviseli-e.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Index vagy random key local state-et és focus-t vihet rossz sorra, vagy minden itemet remountolhat.

## Hogyan működik?

- A data stabil identifierét használd.
- A key siblingek között legyen unique.
- Key cseréje szándékosan resetelheti a state-et.
- Index key csak static listánál safe.

## Gyakori hibák

- Reorderelhető listán indexet használni.
- Render közben random key-t generálni.
- Azt várni, hogy a child normál propként megkapja a key-t.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- key — stabil sibling identity
- remount — component instance lecserélése
- positional identity — pozícióból számított identity

topics.name: State Ownership and Placement
topics.description: Choosing the correct home for local, shared, server, and navigation state.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

State ownership identifies which component or subsystem is responsible for a value. Local UI, shared client, server, and navigation state have different lifecycles.

## What problem does it solve?

Putting everything in one global store increases coupling; keeping shared data too local creates duplicated sources of truth.

## How it works

- Keep state close to the UI that uses it.
- Lift only to the nearest common owner.
- Use Context for stable subtree-wide values.
- Use a query cache for remote data.
- Put shareable filters and pagination in the URL.
- Use a store only for complex shared client state.

## Common mistakes

- Storing every modal in Redux.
- Copying server data into several states.
- Keeping shareable navigation state only in memory.

## Key terms

- source of truth — authoritative owner
- server state — remote data with freshness concerns
- navigation state — state represented by the URL

hu.name: State Ownership és Elhelyezés
hu.description: Local, shared, server és navigation state megfelelő helyének kiválasztása.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A state ownership megmondja, mely component vagy subsystem felel egy value-ért. A local UI, shared client, server és navigation state lifecycle-ja eltér.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Ha minden global store-ba kerül, nő a coupling; ha shared data túl local marad, duplicated source of truth keletkezik.

## Hogyan működik?

- A state maradjon közel a használó UI-hoz.
- Csak a nearest common ownerhez lifteld.
- Stabil subtree-wide value-hoz Context jó.
- Remote data query cache-be való.
- Shareable filter és pagination kerüljön URL-be.
- Store csak complex shared client state esetén kell.

## Gyakori hibák

- Minden modalt Reduxban tárolni.
- Server data-t több state-be bemásolni.
- Shareable navigation state-et csak memoryban tartani.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- source of truth — authoritative owner
- server state — freshness problémát hordozó remote data
- navigation state — URL-ben reprezentált state

topics.name: Hooks and Effects
topics.description: Composing stateful behaviour and synchronizing React with external systems.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Hooks compose stateful behaviour in function components. Effects synchronize with systems React does not own, such as subscriptions, timers, network requests, DOM APIs, or third-party libraries.

## What problem does it solve?

Conditional Hook calls break state association, while effects used as general control flow create loops, stale closures, races, and duplicated state.

## How it works

- Call Hooks only at the top level of components or custom Hooks.
- Keep Hook order stable.
- Include every reactive effect dependency.
- Return cleanup for subscriptions, timers, listeners, and obsolete work.
- Derive values during render instead of syncing duplicates.
- Make setup safe under repeated mount-cleanup-mount checks.

## Common mistakes

- Calling Hooks conditionally.
- Omitting dependencies to force fewer runs.
- Starting subscriptions without cleanup.
- Copying props into state through an effect without need.

## Key terms

- custom Hook — reusable Hook composition
- dependency array — reactive effect inputs
- cleanup — undo previous synchronization
- stale closure — callback reading old logical state

hu.name: Hooks és Effects
hu.description: Stateful behaviour composition és Reacten kívüli rendszerekkel való synchronization.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A Hook stateful behaviourt compose-ol function componentben. Az effect olyan rendszerrel szinkronizál, amelyet React nem ownol, például subscription, timer, network request, DOM API vagy third-party library.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Conditional Hook call eltöri a state associationt, a general control flow-ként használt effect pedig loopot, stale closure-t, race-t és duplicated state-et okoz.

## Hogyan működik?

- Hook csak component vagy custom Hook top leveljén fusson.
- A Hook order legyen stabil.
- Minden reactive effect dependency szerepeljen.
- Subscription, timer, listener és obsolete work kapjon cleanupot.
- Derivable value render közben készüljön.
- Setup legyen safe repeated mount-cleanup-mount alatt.

## Gyakori hibák

- Hookot conditionben hívni.
- Dependencyt kihagyni, hogy ritkábban fusson.
- Subscriptiont cleanup nélkül indítani.
- Propsot indok nélkül effecttel state-be másolni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- custom Hook — reusable Hook composition
- dependency array — reactive effect input
- cleanup — korábbi synchronization visszavonása
- stale closure — régi logical state-et olvasó callback

topics.name: React Memoization and Profiling
topics.description: Measuring render cost and applying memoization or virtualization deliberately.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

React memoization can skip a component render or reuse a calculation when inputs and references remain equal. Profiling identifies whether rendering is a meaningful bottleneck.

## What problem does it solve?

Memoizing everything adds complexity and often fails when parents create new object or function references each render.

## How it works

- Measure with React Profiler and browser tools first.
- Reduce state scope and split components before adding caches.
- Use memo for expensive children with usually equal props.
- Use useMemo for expensive calculations and useCallback when function identity matters.
- Treat caches as performance hints.
- Virtualize very large lists.

## Common mistakes

- Memoizing every value and function.
- Omitting dependencies to preserve identity.
- Passing fresh objects to memoized children.
- Using useMemo for correctness or side effects.

## Key terms

- stable reference — same identity across renders
- memo — component render optimization
- profiling — measurement of render work
- virtualization — mounting only visible rows

hu.name: React Memoization és Profiling
hu.description: Render cost mérése, majd targeted memoization vagy virtualization.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A React memoization kihagyhat component rendert vagy újrahasználhat calculationt, ha input és reference unchanged. A profiling mutatja meg, hogy a render valóban bottleneck-e.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Mindenhol memoization complexityt hoz, és nem segít, ha a parent minden renderben új object vagy function reference-et ad.

## Hogyan működik?

- Először React Profilerrel és browser toollal mérj.
- Cache előtt csökkentsd a state scope-ot és bontsd a componentet.
- memo expensive childhoz jó equal props mellett.
- useMemo expensive calculationhoz, useCallback fontos function identityhez kell.
- A cache performance hint.
- Nagy listát virtualizálj.

## Gyakori hibák

- Minden value-t és functiont memoizálni.
- Identityért dependencyt kihagyni.
- Memoized childnak fresh objectet adni.
- useMemo-t correctnessre vagy side effectre használni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- stable reference — ugyanaz az identity renderek között
- memo — component render optimization
- profiling — render work mérése
- virtualization — csak visible row mountolása

topics.name: Controlled and Uncontrolled Forms
topics.description: Choosing whether React state or the DOM owns input values and validation flow.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

A controlled input gets its value from React state. An uncontrolled input keeps its value in the DOM and is read through a ref or submission.

## What problem does it solve?

Forms need validation, error association, reset, browser behaviour, and performance. Mixing ownership causes warnings and inconsistent values.

## How it works

- Use controlled fields when UI behaviour depends on every change.
- Use uncontrolled fields for simpler forms or suitable form libraries.
- Keep one owner per field.
- Preserve input after recoverable errors.
- Associate labels and field errors.
- Model submitting, success, and failure explicitly.

## Common mistakes

- Switching unexpectedly between controlled and uncontrolled.
- Clearing input after a server error.
- Showing one generic error without field association.

## Key terms

- controlled input — React owns the value
- uncontrolled input — DOM owns the value
- field error — validation tied to one input

hu.name: Controlled és Uncontrolled Forms
hu.description: Annak kiválasztása, hogy React state vagy a DOM ownolja az input value-t.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Controlled input value-ja React state-ből jön. Uncontrolled input az aktuális value-t a DOM-ban tartja, és ref vagy submission segítségével olvasható.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A form validationt, error associationt, resetet, browser behaviourt és performance-et kezel. Ownership keverése warningot és inkonzisztens value-t okoz.

## Hogyan működik?

- Controlled field jó, ha minden change befolyásolja a UI-t.
- Uncontrolled field egyszerű formnál vagy megfelelő form librarynél jó lehet.
- Egy fieldnek egy ownerje legyen.
- Recoverable error után maradjon meg az input.
- Label és field error kapcsolódjon.
- Submitting, success és failure legyen explicit.

## Gyakori hibák

- Controlled és uncontrolled state között váratlanul váltani.
- Server error után inputot törölni.
- Egy generic errort mutatni field association nélkül.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- controlled input — React ownolja a value-t
- uncontrolled input — DOM ownolja a value-t
- field error — egy inputhoz kötött validation

topics.name: Server State and Query Caches
topics.description: Managing remote data with freshness, caching, refetching, and invalidation.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Server state is owned by a remote system. Query libraries manage caching, request deduplication, stale data, refetching, retries, and invalidation.

## What problem does it solve?

Treating API responses as ordinary client state forces teams to rebuild freshness, races, and cache invalidation manually.

## How it works

- Identify queries with stable keys including relevant parameters.
- Define stale time and cache lifetime.
- Invalidate or update affected queries after mutations.
- Keep transient UI state outside the query cache.
- Distinguish initial loading from background refetching.
- Do not retry permanent permission or validation errors.

## Common mistakes

- Copying query data into local state without need.
- Reusing one key for different parameters.
- Treating cached data as permanently authoritative.

## Key terms

- query key — cache identifier
- stale data — cached data eligible for refetch
- invalidation — marking cached data outdated
- background refetch — refresh while old data remains visible

hu.name: Server State és Query Cache
hu.description: Remote data kezelése freshness, cache, refetch és invalidation segítségével.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A server state remote rendszer által owned data. A query library cache-t, request deduplicationt, stale data-t, refetch-et, retryt és invalidationt kezel.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Ha API response ordinary client state-ként van kezelve, a team kézzel építi újra a freshness, race és cache invalidation mechanizmust.

## Hogyan működik?

- Query stable keyt kapjon minden releváns parameterrel.
- Stale time és cache lifetime legyen definiálva.
- Mutation után az érintett query invalidate vagy update történjen.
- Transient UI state ne kerüljön query cache-be.
- Initial loading és background refetch legyen külön.
- Permission és validation errort ne retryolj automatikusan.

## Gyakori hibák

- Query data-t indok nélkül local state-be másolni.
- Külön parameterhez ugyanazt a key-t használni.
- Cached data-t örökké authoritative-nak tekinteni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- query key — cache identifier
- stale data — refetchre jogosult cached data
- invalidation — cache outdatedként jelölése
- background refetch — refresh régi data mellett

topics.name: Async UI States and Optimistic Updates
topics.description: Designing loading, error, empty, success, pending, and rollback behaviour.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Async UI needs intentional loading, error, empty, success, and sometimes optimistic pending states. Optimistic UI shows an expected result before server confirmation and reconciles later.

## What problem does it solve?

Blank screens, generic errors, stale responses, and dishonest success make the app feel unreliable.

## How it works

- Use skeletons when preserving layout helps.
- Distinguish validation, permission, not-found, network, and server failures.
- Treat empty as guidance, not an error.
- Prevent old responses from overwriting new results.
- Use optimistic updates only for high-success, low-risk, reversible actions.
- Roll back or expose retry on failure.

## Common mistakes

- Building only the happy path.
- Showing one error for every failure.
- Treating empty as failure.
- Pretending payment succeeded before confirmation.

## Key terms

- skeleton — loading placeholder preserving layout
- empty state — no-data guidance
- optimistic update — local change before confirmation
- rollback — undo failed optimistic state

hu.name: Async UI State és Optimistic Update
hu.description: Loading, error, empty, success, pending és rollback behaviour tervezése.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Async UI-hoz intentional loading, error, empty, success és néha optimistic pending state kell. Optimistic UI server confirmation előtt mutatja a várható resultot, majd reconcile-ol.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Blank screen, generic error, stale response és dishonest success miatt az app unreliable-nak tűnik.

## Hogyan működik?

- Skeleton akkor jó, ha a layout helyét meg kell tartani.
- Validation, permission, not-found, network és server failure különüljön el.
- Az empty guidance, nem error.
- Old response ne írja felül az új resultot.
- Optimistic update csak high-success, low-risk, reversible actionnél legyen.
- Failure esetén rollback vagy retry kell.

## Gyakori hibák

- Csak happy pathot építeni.
- Minden failure-re ugyanazt az errort adni.
- Empty state-et failure-nek kezelni.
- Paymentet confirmation előtt sikeresnek mutatni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- skeleton — layoutot tartó loading placeholder
- empty state — no-data guidance
- optimistic update — local change confirmation előtt
- rollback — failed optimistic state visszavonása

questions.title: A React page re-renders too much
questions.prompt: A page feels slow and many components re-render. Walk through how you would investigate and optimize it.
questions.answerStructureHint: Measure first, find the trigger, apply structural fixes, then targeted memoization or virtualization.

expectedPoints.label: Measures first
expectedPoints.description: Uses React Profiler or browser tooling to locate expensive work.

expectedPoints.label: Finds the trigger
expectedPoints.description: Checks state scope, parent renders, Context, selectors, and unstable props.

expectedPoints.label: Applies structural fixes
expectedPoints.description: Localizes state, splits components, and narrows subscriptions.

expectedPoints.label: Uses targeted optimization
expectedPoints.description: Uses memo, useMemo, useCallback, or virtualization for a measured cause.

followUps.prompt: Why can React.memo fail when the parent passes an inline object or function?

hu.title: Túl sok React re-render vizsgálata
hu.prompt: Egy page lassú, és sok component re-renderel. Hogyan vizsgálnád és optimalizálnád?
hu.answerStructureHint: Measurement, trigger, structural fix, majd targeted memoization vagy virtualization.

react_perf_measure.label: Először mér
react_perf_measure.description: React Profiler vagy browser tool alapján keresi az expensive worköt.

react_perf_trigger.label: Megkeresi a triggert
react_perf_trigger.description: State scope, parent render, Context, selector és unstable props kerül elő.

react_perf_structure.label: Strukturális javítást alkalmaz
react_perf_structure.description: State localization, component split és narrow subscription szerepel.

react_perf_target.label: Targeted optimizationt használ
react_perf_target.description: memo, useMemo, useCallback vagy virtualization csak mért okra kerül be.

followUps.react_perf_fu: Miért nem segít a React.memo, ha a parent inline objectet vagy functiont ad?

questions.title: List state moves to the wrong row
questions.prompt: After inserting an item at the top of a list, input values and focus appear in the wrong rows. What is the likely cause and fix?
questions.answerStructureHint: Explain positional identity, stable keys, and why random keys are also wrong.

expectedPoints.label: Identifies unstable keys
expectedPoints.description: Recognizes array indexes or changing keys as the cause.

expectedPoints.label: Explains identity
expectedPoints.description: Explains that React associates state and DOM with type and key.

expectedPoints.label: Uses stable IDs
expectedPoints.description: Uses an identifier belonging to the logical item.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids random keys
expectedPoints.description: Notes that random keys remount rows and lose state.

followUps.prompt: When would deliberately changing a key be useful?

hu.title: A list state rossz sorra kerül
hu.prompt: Egy item beszúrása után az input value és focus rossz sorokban jelenik meg. Mi az ok és javítás?
hu.answerStructureHint: Positional identity, stable key és random key problémája.

react_key_cause.label: Felismeri az unstable key-t
react_key_cause.description: Array index vagy változó key okozza az identity problémát.

react_key_identity.label: Elmagyarázza az identityt
react_key_identity.description: React type és key alapján társít state-et és DOM-ot.

react_key_fix.label: Stabil ID-t használ
react_key_fix.description: A logikai item persistent identifierje lesz a key.

react_key_random.label: Elkerüli a random key-t
react_key_random.description: Random key remountot és state loss-t okoz.

followUps.react_key_fu: Mikor lehet hasznos a key szándékos megváltoztatása?

questions.title: Everything is stored globally
questions.prompt: Modal state, API responses, filters, user session, and form fields all live in one store. What stays and what moves?
questions.answerStructureHint: Classify local, shared, server, and navigation state, then place each by lifecycle.

expectedPoints.label: Classifies state
expectedPoints.description: Separates local UI, shared client, server, and navigation state.

expectedPoints.label: Moves local state closer
expectedPoints.description: Keeps modal and isolated form state near the owning UI.

expectedPoints.label: Uses a query cache
expectedPoints.description: Uses server-state tooling for caching, freshness, refetch, and invalidation.

expectedPoints.label: Uses URL state
expectedPoints.description: Places shareable filters and pagination in the URL.

followUps.prompt: Where would Context fit after this cleanup?

hu.title: Minden state global store-ban van
hu.prompt: Modal, API response, filter, user session és form field is egy store-ban él. Mi maradna és mit mozgatnál?
hu.answerStructureHint: Local, shared, server és navigation state osztályozása lifecycle alapján.

react_state_class.label: Osztályozza a state-et
react_state_class.description: Local UI, shared client, server és navigation state külön kategória.

react_state_local.label: Local state-et közelebb viszi
react_state_local.description: Modal és isolated form state a használó UI közelébe kerül.

react_state_server.label: Query cache-t használ
react_state_server.description: Caching, freshness, refetch és invalidation server-state tool feladata.

react_state_url.label: URL state-et használ
react_state_url.description: Shareable filter és pagination URL-ben él.

followUps.react_state_fu: Hol lenne helye a Contextnek a cleanup után?

questions.title: An effect creates duplicate subscriptions
questions.prompt: In development, a component subscribes twice and receives duplicate events. The team wants to disable StrictMode. What is the better response?
questions.answerStructureHint: Treat repeated setup as a test, make setup safe, add cleanup, and verify dependencies.

expectedPoints.label: Understands the development check
expectedPoints.description: Explains that repeated setup exposes unsafe effects rather than normal production duplication.

expectedPoints.label: Adds cleanup
expectedPoints.description: Unsubscribes or destroys the external resource before rerun and unmount.

expectedPoints.label: Checks dependencies
expectedPoints.description: Includes reactive inputs and avoids unstable accidental resubscription.

expectedPoints.label: Makes setup safe
expectedPoints.description: Ensures repeated setup and cleanup leave the external system consistent.

followUps.prompt: How would you prevent an older fetch response from updating after parameters change?

hu.title: Egy effect duplicate subscriptiont hoz létre
hu.prompt: Developmentben egy component kétszer subscribe-ol. A team kikapcsolná a StrictMode-ot. Mi a jobb megoldás?
hu.answerStructureHint: Repeated setup mint test, safe setup, cleanup és helyes dependency.

react_eff_check.label: Érti a development checket
react_eff_check.description: A repeated setup unsafe effectet tár fel, nem normál production duplication.

react_eff_cleanup.label: Cleanupot ad
react_eff_cleanup.description: Rerun és unmount előtt unsubscribe vagy resource destroy történik.

react_eff_deps.label: Ellenőrzi a dependencyket
react_eff_deps.description: Reactive input szerepel, unstable dependency nem okoz accidental resubscriptiont.

react_eff_safe.label: Safe setupot készít
react_eff_safe.description: Repeated setup és cleanup után az external system konzisztens marad.

followUps.react_eff_fu: Hogyan akadályoznád meg, hogy parameter change után régebbi fetch response update-eljen?

questions.title: Designing a robust form
questions.prompt: A form loses user input after a server error and reports every validation failure in one banner. How would you redesign it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Preserve values, associate errors with fields, model submission states, and keep one ownership model.

expectedPoints.label: Preserves input
expectedPoints.description: Keeps entered values after recoverable validation or server failures.

expectedPoints.label: Associates field errors
expectedPoints.description: Maps structured errors to labelled fields and useful focus behaviour.

expectedPoints.label: Models submission states
expectedPoints.description: Separates idle, submitting, success, validation failure, and server failure.

expectedPoints.label: Uses consistent ownership
expectedPoints.description: Chooses controlled, uncontrolled, or a form library deliberately.

followUps.prompt: How would you prevent accidental double submission?

hu.title: Robusztus form tervezése
hu.prompt: Egy form server error után elveszíti a user inputot, és minden validation failure-t egy bannerben mutat. Hogyan terveznéd újra?
hu.answerStructureHint: Value preservation, field error association, submission state és egyértelmű ownership.

react_form_keep.label: Megőrzi a user inputot
react_form_keep.description: Recoverable failure után a beírt value megmarad.

react_form_field.label: Fieldhez kapcsolja az errort
react_form_field.description: Structured error a megfelelő labelled fieldhez és focus behaviourhöz kapcsolódik.

react_form_states.label: Modellezi a submission state-eket
react_form_states.description: Idle, submitting, success, validation failure és server failure külön állapot.

react_form_owner.label: Konzisztens ownershipot használ
react_form_owner.description: Controlled, uncontrolled vagy form library tudatos választás.

followUps.react_form_fu: Hogyan akadályoznád meg az accidental double submissiont?

questions.title: The design contains only the happy path
questions.prompt: You receive a list-screen design that only shows successful data. What additional states and backend contract details do you need?
questions.answerStructureHint: Cover loading, empty, error categories, recovery, stale requests, and machine-readable errors.

expectedPoints.label: Names core states
expectedPoints.description: Designs loading, empty, error, and success explicitly.

expectedPoints.label: Separates error categories
expectedPoints.description: Distinguishes validation, permission, not-found, network, and server failures.

expectedPoints.label: Requests a structured contract
expectedPoints.description: Needs stable status and machine-readable error bodies.

expectedPoints.label: Handles timing and races
expectedPoints.description: Avoids flashing loaders and prevents stale responses from overwriting newer results.

followUps.prompt: Which actions would be suitable for optimistic UI?

hu.title: A design csak happy pathot tartalmaz
hu.prompt: Egy list screen design csak successful data-t mutat. Milyen state és backend contract detail kell még?
hu.answerStructureHint: Loading, empty, error category, recovery, stale request és machine-readable error.

fe_states_core.label: Megnevezi a core state-eket
fe_states_core.description: Loading, empty, error és success explicit design része.

fe_states_errors.label: Elkülöníti az error categoryket
fe_states_errors.description: Validation, permission, not-found, network és server failure külön kezelést kap.

fe_states_contract.label: Structured contractot kér
fe_states_contract.description: Stabil status és machine-readable error body alapján választ UI-t.

fe_states_race.label: Kezeli a timingot és race-t
fe_states_race.description: Nem villog a loader, és stale response nem írja felül az új resultot.

followUps.fe_states_fu: Mely action lenne alkalmas optimistic UI-ra?
